5.6.1.value: DNS query volume trending supports capacity planning and reveals traffic pattern changes.

5.6.2.title: NXDOMAIN Spike Detection
5.6.2.value: NXDOMAIN spikes indicate DGA malware (generating random domain lookups), misconfiguration, or DNS infrastructure issues.

5.6.3.title: SERVFAIL Rate Monitoring
5.6.3.value: SERVFAIL increases indicate upstream DNS failures, DNSSEC validation issues, or resolver problems.

5.6.4.title: DNS Tunneling Detection
5.6.4.value: DNS tunneling uses DNS queries to exfiltrate data or establish C2 channels, bypassing traditional security controls.

5.6.5.title: DHCP Scope Exhaustion
5.6.5.value: Empty DHCP scopes prevent new devices from getting network access.

5.6.6.title: DHCP Rogue Server Detection
5.6.6.value: Rogue DHCP servers assign wrong IPs/gateways, causing network disruption and potential MitM attacks.

5.6.7.title: DNS Record Change Audit
5.6.7.value: Unauthorized DNS changes can redirect traffic to attacker infrastructure (DNS hijacking).

5.6.8.title: DNS Latency Monitoring
5.6.8.value: DNS latency directly adds to every network connection. Slow DNS = slow everything.

5.6.9.title: DNS Cache Hit Ratio
5.6.9.value: Low cache hit ratios indicate either a surge of new queries, cache poisoning attempts, or misconfigured TTLs — all increasing latency and upstream load.

5.6.10.title: DNSSEC Validation Failures
5.6.10.value: DNSSEC failures can indicate DNS spoofing attempts or misconfigured zones. Monitoring prevents users from being directed to malicious sites.

5.6.11.title: DHCP Lease Duration Analysis
5.6.11.value: Short lease durations increase DHCP traffic and scope churn. Long leases waste addresses. Optimizing lease times improves IP management.

5.6.12.title: DNS Query Type Distribution
5.6.12.value: Unusual query type distribution (spikes in TXT, MX, or ANY) can indicate DNS tunneling, reconnaissance, or abuse.

5.7.1.title: Top Talkers Analysis
5.7.1.value: Identifies top bandwidth consumers. Essential for troubleshooting congestion and capacity planning.

5.7.2.title: Anomalous Traffic Patterns
5.7.2.value: Unusual flows (new protocols, unexpected destinations) indicate compromise, misconfiguration, or shadow IT.

5.7.3.title: Bandwidth by Application
5.7.3.value: Application-level bandwidth breakdown helps prioritize QoS policies and justify network upgrades.

5.7.4.title: East-West Traffic Monitoring
5.7.4.value: Lateral traffic between internal segments reveals application dependencies and detects lateral movement.

5.7.5.title: Data Exfiltration Detection
5.7.5.value: Unusually large outbound transfers to uncommon destinations may be data theft.

5.7.6.title: Port Scan Detection
5.7.6.value: Hosts scanning many ports on targets indicate reconnaissance, worm propagation, or vulnerability scanning.

5.7.7.title: Protocol Distribution Analysis
5.7.7.value: Understanding protocol mix helps validate network policies and detect unauthorized protocols (e.g., unexpected SSH, RDP, or P2P traffic).

5.7.8.title: Multicast Traffic Monitoring
5.7.8.value: Uncontrolled multicast traffic floods switches and consumes bandwidth. Monitoring ensures multicast storms are detected before impacting unicast traffic.

5.7.9.title: Unauthorized VLAN Traffic Detection
5.7.9.value: Traffic originating from or destined to unauthorized VLANs indicates misconfigured switch ports, VLAN hopping attacks, or rogue devices.

5.7.10.title: Long-Duration Flow Detection
5.7.10.value: Extremely long-lived flows may indicate data exfiltration, persistent backdoors, or stuck sessions consuming resources.

5.8.1.title: DNA Center Assurance Alerts
5.8.1.value: DNA Center provides AI/ML-driven network issue detection. Centralizing in Splunk enables cross-domain correlation.

5.8.2.title: Meraki Organization Monitoring
5.8.2.value: Tracks Meraki device status across all networks and organizations from a single pane.

5.8.3.title: SNMP Trap Consolidation
5.8.3.value: Centralizing SNMP traps from all sources enables cross-tool correlation and reduces monitoring tool sprawl.

5.8.4.title: Network Device Inventory
5.8.4.value: Up-to-date inventory supports change management, vulnerability tracking, and compliance auditing.

5.8.5.title: Network Device Backup Compliance
5.8.5.value: Missing backups mean a failed device requires manual rebuilding. Tracking backup success ensures rapid disaster recovery.

5.8.6.title: ISE Endpoint Posture Compliance
5.8.6.value: Non-compliant endpoints (missing patches, disabled AV) on the network increase attack surface. ISE posture data enables enforcement visibility.

5.8.7.title: Network Configuration Drift Detection
5.8.7.value: Configuration drift from golden standards introduces vulnerabilities and operational inconsistencies. Detecting drift maintains compliance.

5.8.8.title: SNMP Polling Gap Detection
5.8.8.value: Missing SNMP polls create gaps in monitoring data. Detecting polling failures ensures metrics dashboards remain accurate.

5.9.1.title: Wireless Client Association Failures
5.9.1.value: Identifies recurring authentication failures and SSID configuration issues that prevent users from connecting to wireless networks.

5.9.2.title: RSSI/Signal Strength Degradation Detection
5.9.2.value: Proactively identifies weak WiFi coverage areas and client placement issues before users experience connectivity problems.

5.9.3.title: Excessive Client Roaming Activity
5.9.3.value: Detects unstable roaming patterns and AP handoff issues that cause latency spikes and dropped connections.

5.9.4.title: SSID Performance Ranking and Trend Analysis
5.9.4.value: Compares performance across multiple SSIDs to identify underperforming networks and optimize deployment strategy.

5.9.5.title: WiFi Channel Utilization and Interference Detection
5.9.5.value: Identifies channel congestion and interference sources to optimize channel assignments and reduce co-channel interference.

5.9.6.title: Rogue and Unauthorized AP Detection (Air Marshal)
5.9.6.value: Identifies unauthorized wireless networks and malicious APs that may represent security threats or network intrusion attempts.

5.9.7.title: Client Device Type Distribution and Compliance
5.9.7.value: Tracks device types connecting to network for capacity planning, security policy enforcement, and support optimization.

5.9.8.title: Band Steering Effectiveness Assessment
5.9.8.value: Measures effectiveness of steering clients from 2.4GHz to 5GHz bands to reduce congestion and improve performance.

5.9.9.title: Failed DHCP Assignments and IP Pool Exhaustion
5.9.9.value: Detects DHCP server failures and IP pool exhaustion that prevent new clients from obtaining addresses.

5.9.10.title: 802.1X Authentication Failures and Radius Issues
5.9.10.value: Identifies authentication server problems, credential issues, and 802.1X configuration mismatches.

5.9.11.title: DNS Resolution Performance and Failures
5.9.11.value: Monitors DNS query resolution times and failures to identify misconfiguration or server issues affecting user experience.

5.9.12.title: Wireless Latency Analysis by SSID and Location
5.9.12.value: Identifies latency patterns across network to optimize AP placement, channel allocation, and client routing.

5.9.13.title: Splash Page Engagement and Redirection Analytics
5.9.13.value: Tracks guest network splash page performance and user acceptance rates for marketing and network access purposes.

5.9.14.title: Multicast and Broadcast Storm Detection
5.9.14.value: Identifies multicast/broadcast flooding that degrades wireless performance across multiple client devices.

5.9.15.title: Wireless Health Score Trending
5.9.15.value: Provides a composite health metric across all APs to facilitate executive reporting and trend analysis.

5.9.16.title: Connected Client Count Trending and Capacity Planning
5.9.16.value: Tracks client density by AP and SSID for capacity planning and performance optimization.

5.9.17.title: Top Talker Analysis and Bandwidth Hogs
5.9.17.value: Identifies bandwidth-intensive clients and applications to enforce QoS policies and prevent network congestion.

5.9.18.title: Connection Duration and Session Quality
5.9.18.value: Analyzes typical session lengths and stability to identify problematic SSIDs or time-based issues.

5.9.19.title: AP Uptime and Availability Monitoring
5.9.19.value: Ensures all access points are online and operational; alerts on unexpected AP outages.

5.9.20.title: Mesh Network Link Quality and Backhaul Health
5.9.20.value: Monitors wireless mesh backhaul links to ensure reliability of remote AP connections.

5.9.21.title: Guest Network Access Patterns and Usage
5.9.21.value: Tracks guest network adoption, usage patterns, and peak times for network provisioning.

5.9.22.title: WiFi Geolocation and Location Analytics
5.9.22.value: Uses Cisco Meraki location services to track foot traffic patterns and heat maps in physical spaces.

5.9.23.title: Port Utilization and Congestion Alerts
5.9.23.value: Identifies port saturation and congestion events that require capacity upgrades or load balancing adjustments.

5.9.24.title: Power over Ethernet (PoE) Consumption Tracking
5.9.24.value: Monitors PoE power allocation to prevent over-subscription and ensure sufficient power for all devices.

5.9.25.title: Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) Topology Changes
5.9.25.value: Alerts on unexpected STP topology changes that indicate link failures or network configuration issues.

5.9.26.title: Port Security Violations and Rogue Device Detection
5.9.26.value: Detects unauthorized MAC addresses and port security breaches that indicate potential network intrusion.

5.9.27.title: Switch Interface Up/Down Events and Link Flapping
5.9.27.value: Identifies port flapping, cable issues, and unstable link states that cause intermittent connectivity.

5.9.28.title: VLAN Configuration Mismatches and Tagging Violations
5.9.28.value: Detects VLAN configuration errors and tagging violations that disrupt network segmentation.

5.9.29.title: MAC Flooding and Bridge Table Exhaustion
5.9.29.value: Detects MAC address table exhaustion and flooding attacks that could overwhelm switch resources.

5.9.30.title: DHCP Snooping Violations
5.9.30.value: Detects unauthorized DHCP servers and spoofing attempts that disrupt network address allocation.

5.9.31.title: Broadcast Storm Detection and Mitigation
5.9.31.value: Identifies and alerts on broadcast storms that can freeze network performance across all switches.

5.9.32.title: Switch CPU and Memory Utilization
5.9.32.value: Monitors switch hardware resources to prevent performance degradation or device failure.

5.9.33.title: Stack Unit and Redundancy Health
5.9.33.value: Ensures switch stacking configuration remains healthy and redundancy is not compromised.

5.9.34.title: Trunk Link Utilization and Performance
5.9.34.value: Monitors inter-switch and uplink trunk utilization to identify bandwidth constraints.

5.9.35.title: QoS Queue Drops and Priority Violations
5.9.35.value: Detects QoS queue overflow and drops that indicate traffic priority issues.

5.9.36.title: Port Access Control List (ACL) Hits and Block Events
5.9.36.value: Tracks ACL rule hits to monitor policy enforcement and identify anomalous traffic.

5.9.37.title: Cable Test Results and Port Diagnostics
5.9.37.value: Analyzes cable integrity test results to identify wiring faults before they cause outages.

5.9.38.title: Uplink Health and Failover Events
5.9.38.value: Monitors primary/secondary uplink status to detect failover events and connection issues.

5.9.39.title: VPN Tunnel Status and Path Monitoring
5.9.39.value: Ensures all site-to-site and client VPN tunnels remain active and operative.

5.9.40.title: Content Filtering and URL Category Blocks

6.3.2.title: Backup Job Duration Trending
6.3.2.value: Increasing backup durations signal data growth, network congestion, or storage performance issues. Prevents backup window overruns.

6.3.3.title: Missed Backup Detection
6.3.3.value: A backup that doesn't run at all is worse than one that fails — it's invisible. Detection ensures no system is left unprotected.

6.3.4.title: Backup Storage Capacity

6.3.5.title: Restore Test Tracking
6.3.5.value: Backups are worthless if restores fail. Tracking restore tests ensures confidence in recoverability and satisfies audit requirements.

6.3.6.title: Backup SLA Compliance

6.3.7.title: Backup Data Volume Trending
6.3.7.value: Tracks data growth rate for capacity planning of backup infrastructure. Identifies unexpected data surges early.

6.3.8.title: Tape Library Health

6.4.1.title: File Access Audit

6.4.2.title: Ransomware Indicator Detection

6.4.3.title: DFS Replication Health

7.1.1.title: Slow Query Detection
7.1.1.value: Slow queries degrade application performance and user experience. Identifying them enables targeted optimization.

7.1.2.title: Deadlock Monitoring
7.1.2.value: Deadlocks cause transaction failures and application errors. Rapid detection and root cause analysis minimizes impact.

7.1.3.title: Connection Pool Exhaustion
7.1.3.value: Exhausted connection pools cause application failures. Monitoring prevents outages and guides pool sizing decisions.

7.1.4.title: Replication Lag Monitoring
7.1.4.value: Replication lag affects data consistency and failover readiness. Monitoring ensures HA/DR objectives are met.

7.1.5.title: Tablespace / Data File Growth
7.1.5.value: Uncontrolled database growth leads to disk space exhaustion and outages. Trending enables proactive storage provisioning.

7.1.6.title: Backup Success Verification

7.1.7.title: Login Failure Monitoring
7.1.7.value: Repeated login failures may indicate brute-force attacks or misconfigured applications. Detection supports security posture.

7.1.8.title: Long-Running Transaction Detection
7.1.8.value: Long transactions hold locks, causing blocking chains that degrade application performance for many users.

7.1.9.title: Index Fragmentation
7.1.9.value: Highly fragmented indexes cause excessive I/O and slow query performance. Monitoring guides maintenance scheduling.

7.1.10.title: TempDB Contention (SQL Server)

7.1.11.title: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio
7.1.11.value: Low buffer cache hit ratio means excessive disk I/O. Monitoring guides memory allocation decisions.

7.1.12.title: Database Availability Group Health

7.1.13.title: Schema Change Detection

7.1.14.title: Query Plan Regression
7.1.14.value: Query plan changes can cause sudden performance degradation. Detection enables rapid intervention (plan forcing, hint application).

7.1.15.title: Privilege Escalation Audit
7.1.15.value: Unauthorized privilege changes can enable data theft or sabotage. Audit trail is required for compliance.

7.2.1.title: Cluster Membership Changes
7.2.1.value: Node additions/removals affect data distribution and availability. Unexpected membership changes may indicate failures.

7.2.2.title: Replication Lag / Consistency
7.2.2.value: Replication lag causes stale reads and eventual consistency violations. Monitoring ensures data freshness SLAs are met.

7.2.3.title: Read/Write Latency Trending
7.2.3.value: Latency trending detects performance degradation before it impacts users. Enables proactive tuning and scaling decisions.

7.2.4.title: Shard Imbalance Detection
7.2.4.value: Uneven shard distribution causes hot spots and performance inconsistency. Rebalancing prevents overloaded nodes.

7.2.5.title: Compaction Monitoring
7.2.5.value: Pending compactions consume I/O and can cause write amplification. Monitoring ensures compaction keeps pace with writes.

7.2.6.title: GC Pause Detection
7.2.6.value: Long GC pauses in Java-based databases (Cassandra, Elasticsearch) cause request timeouts and can trigger node eviction from the cluster.

7.2.7.title: Connection Count Monitoring
7.2.7.value: Approaching connection limits causes client rejections. Monitoring enables proactive limit adjustment or connection pooling.

7.2.8.title: Index Build Monitoring
7.2.8.value: Index builds consume significant resources and can impact production performance. Tracking ensures builds complete within maintenance windows.

7.2.9.title: Memory Utilization

7.2.10.title: Elasticsearch Cluster Health

7.3.3.title: Read Replica Lag

7.3.4.title: Storage Auto-Scaling Events

7.3.5.title: Maintenance Window Tracking

7.4.5.title: Warehouse Utilization
7.4.5.value: Right-sizing warehouses reduces cost while maintaining performance. Utilization data drives scaling policy decisions.

7.1.16.title: Open Cursor Leak Detection
7.1.16.value: Open cursors that are never closed accumulate in the database session context and eventually exhaust the cursor limit (Oracle ORA-01000, SQL Server max open cursors), causing application errors and forcing emergency restarts. Nagios detects this via threshold checks on V$OPEN_CURSOR; Splunk enables trending, per-session attribution, and correlation with application deployments.

8.1.1.title: HTTP Error Rate Monitoring
8.1.1.value: Rising error rates signal application issues, backend failures, or attacks. Rapid detection reduces user impact and MTTR.

8.1.2.title: Response Time Trending
8.1.2.value: Increasing response times degrade user experience before complete failures occur. Trending enables proactive optimization.

8.1.3.title: Request Rate Trending
8.1.3.value: Traffic trending supports capacity planning and identifies unexpected traffic patterns (bot attacks, viral events, traffic drops).

8.1.4.title: Top Error URIs
8.1.4.value: Identifies the most problematic endpoints for targeted developer attention. Reduces noise by focusing on high-impact errors.

8.1.5.title: SSL Certificate Monitoring
8.1.5.value: Expired SSL certificates cause complete service outage and browser security warnings. Proactive monitoring prevents this entirely avoidable failure.

8.1.6.title: Upstream Backend Health
8.1.6.value: Backend server failures behind reverse proxies cause partial service degradation. Detection enables rapid failover response.

8.1.7.title: Bot and Crawler Detection
8.1.7.value: Bot traffic inflates metrics and consumes resources. Identification enables accurate capacity planning and bot management policies.

8.1.8.title: Connection Pool Saturation
8.1.8.value: Saturated worker threads/processes cause request queuing and timeouts. Monitoring enables proactive scaling.

8.1.9.title: Slow POST Detection
8.1.9.value: Slow POST requests often indicate application-level performance issues (large form submissions, file uploads, database writes) distinct from slow GETs.

8.1.10.title: Configuration Reload Tracking
8.1.10.value: Configuration changes are a common cause of outages. Tracking reloads enables rapid correlation with incidents.

8.2.1.title: JVM Heap Utilization
8.2.1.value: JVM heap exhaustion causes OutOfMemoryError, crashing the application. Monitoring enables tuning before failures occur.

8.2.2.title: Garbage Collection Impact
8.2.2.value: Frequent or long GC pauses cause application latency spikes and request timeouts. Monitoring guides JVM tuning.

8.2.3.title: Thread Pool Exhaustion
8.2.3.value: Exhausted thread pools cause request rejection and application unresponsiveness. Detection prevents complete service failure.

8.2.4.title: Application Error Rate
8.2.4.value: Application exceptions indicate bugs, integration failures, or environmental issues. Tracking error rate by type guides debugging priority.

8.2.5.title: Deployment Tracking
8.2.5.value: Correlating deployments with performance changes is the fastest way to identify deployment-caused regressions. Essential for change management.

8.2.6.title: Connection Pool Monitoring
8.2.6.value: Exhausted JDBC/database connection pools cause application errors and cascading failures. Monitoring prevents connection starvation.

8.2.7.title: Session Count Trending
8.2.7.value: Active session counts indicate concurrent user load. Trending supports capacity planning and license management.

8.2.8.title: .NET CLR Performance
8.2.8.value: CLR performance issues (high GC, exceptions, thread starvation) directly impact .NET application performance. Monitoring guides runtime tuning.

8.2.9.title: Node.js Event Loop Lag
8.2.9.value: Event loop lag indicates blocking operations that prevent Node.js from handling requests. Detection enables code-level investigation.

8.2.10.title: Class Loading Issues
8.2.10.value: ClassNotFoundException and NoClassDefFoundError indicate deployment or dependency issues that may cause intermittent failures.

8.3.1.title: Consumer Lag Monitoring
8.3.1.value: Growing consumer lag means messages aren't being processed fast enough, leading to data staleness and eventual message loss if retention is exceeded.

8.3.2.title: Queue Depth Trending
8.3.2.value: Growing queue depths indicate consumers can't keep up or are down. Trending prevents message loss and processing delays.

8.3.3.title: Broker Health Monitoring
8.3.3.value: Broker failures cause message loss and application disruption. Health monitoring ensures cluster stability.

8.3.4.title: Under-Replicated Partitions
8.3.4.value: Under-replicated partitions mean data is at risk of loss if additional brokers fail. Immediate remediation is required.

8.3.5.title: Dead Letter Queue Monitoring
8.3.5.value: Messages in DLQ represent processing failures that need investigation. They may indicate bugs, schema changes, or downstream failures.

8.3.6.title: Message Throughput Trending
8.3.6.value: Throughput trending identifies capacity limits and validates scaling decisions. Unexpected drops indicate producer or broker issues.

8.3.7.title: Topic/Queue Creation Audit
8.3.7.value: Uncontrolled topic/queue creation can lead to resource sprawl. Audit trail supports governance and cleanup.

8.3.8.title: Consumer Group Rebalancing
8.3.8.value: Frequent rebalances cause processing pauses and duplicate message delivery. Detection identifies unstable consumers.

8.3.9.title: Partition Leader Elections
8.3.9.value: Frequent leader elections indicate broker instability, causing temporary unavailability for affected partitions.

8.3.10.title: Message Age Monitoring
8.3.10.value: Old messages in queues indicate processing delays that may violate SLAs. Age tracking provides a business-relevant metric beyond raw queue depth.

8.4.1.title: API Error Rate by Endpoint
8.4.1.value: Per-endpoint error rates pinpoint failing services, enabling targeted debugging rather than broad investigation.

8.4.2.title: API Latency Percentiles
8.4.2.value: P95/P99 latency reveals the experience of the slowest users. Averages hide tail latency problems.

8.4.3.title: Rate Limiting Events
8.4.3.value: Rate limiting indicates consumers exceeding their quotas. May signal API abuse, misconfigured clients, or quota adjustments needed.

8.4.4.title: Authentication Failures
8.4.4.value: Authentication failures may indicate credential compromise, API key rotation issues, or brute-force attacks.

8.4.5.title: Service-to-Service Call Failures
8.4.5.value: Inter-service communication failures in microservices architectures cascade quickly. Detection enables rapid isolation of failing services.

8.4.6.title: Circuit Breaker Activations
8.4.6.value: Circuit breaker trips indicate a downstream service is failing. Quick detection enables proactive communication and remediation.

8.4.7.title: API Consumer Usage Tracking
8.4.7.value: Usage tracking per API consumer enables billing, quota management, and partner relationship management.

8.4.8.title: mTLS Certificate Expiration
8.4.8.value: Expired mTLS certificates break service-to-service communication, causing complete mesh failures. Proactive monitoring is essential.

8.5.1.title: Cache Hit/Miss Ratio
8.5.1.value: Cache hit ratio directly measures cache effectiveness. Declining ratio means more backend load and higher latency.

8.5.2.title: Memory Utilization
8.5.2.value: Cache memory exhaustion triggers evictions, degrading performance. Monitoring enables timely scaling.

8.5.3.title: Eviction Rate Trending
8.5.3.value: High eviction rates mean the cache is too small, causing frequent backend roundtrips. Tracking guides capacity decisions.

8.5.4.title: Connection Count Monitoring
8.5.4.value: Approaching connection limits causes client rejections. Monitoring enables proactive limit adjustment.

8.5.5.title: Replication Lag (Redis)
8.5.5.value: Redis replication lag affects read consistency for apps reading from replicas. Monitoring ensures data freshness.

8.5.6.title: Slow Command Detection
8.5.6.value: Slow Redis commands block the single-threaded event loop, impacting all clients. Detection enables command optimization.

8.5.7.title: Key Expiration Trending
8.5.7.value: Monitoring TTL patterns ensures cache freshness strategy is working. Unusual patterns may indicate application bugs.

8.6.1.title: SSH Service Availability Monitoring
8.6.1.value: SSH is the primary remote administration channel for Linux and Unix servers. An unresponsive SSH daemon locks out operators and often signals broader system distress (OOM, hung kernel, storage full). Nagios `check_ssh` is one of the most universally deployed checks; Splunk replicates it through absence-of-event detection and syslog-based availability trending.

8.6.2.title: FTP / SFTP Service Availability Monitoring
8.6.2.value: FTP and SFTP services support automated file transfer workflows between systems, partners, and legacy integrations. Silent service failures cause missed file deliveries that may not surface until business processes fail downstream. Nagios `check_ftp` provides port-level verification; Splunk replicates this through daemon log monitoring and scripted probes.

8.6.3.title: SMTP Service Availability
8.6.3.value: Distinct from mail queue depth monitoring — this checks whether the SMTP daemon is accepting TCP connections and responding to EHLO. A crashed postfix or sendmail process stops inbound/outbound mail entirely without generating queue entries. Nagios `check_smtp` verifies this at the connection layer; Splunk replicates it via daemon-level log monitoring.

8.6.4.title: POP3 / IMAP Mail Retrieval Service Availability
8.6.4.value: POP3 and IMAP services allow mail clients to retrieve messages. Even when delivery works correctly, a crashed Dovecot or Cyrus daemon prevents users from reading email, appearing as a total mail outage. Nagios `check_pop` and `check_imap` monitor these ports directly; Splunk replicates availability detection through daemon log analysis.

14.3.8.value: Prevents equipment failure by detecting dew point conditions before condensation forms on servers and network infrastructure.

14.3.9.title: Cold Storage Room Temperature Excursion Alert
14.3.9.value: Ensures pharmaceutical, food, or vaccine storage integrity by alerting within minutes of unplanned temperature rise.

14.3.10.title: Museum & Archive Climate Control Compliance
14.3.10.value: Documents preservation requirements (typically 18-21°C, 35-45% RH) for regulatory compliance and insurance.

14.3.11.title: Greenhouse Humidity & Growth Optimization
14.3.11.value: Optimizes plant growth rates by maintaining ideal VPD (vapor pressure deficit) and reducing fungal disease risk.

14.3.12.title: Security Camera Motion Detection with Light Level Correlation
14.3.12.value: Reduces false motion alerts by correlating camera motion events with ambient light levels and eliminating day/night false positives.

14.3.13.title: Energy Management & HVAC Occupancy-Based Control
14.3.13.value: Reduces HVAC energy consumption 15-30% by correlating occupancy detection with temperature setpoints.

14.3.14.title: Warehouse Inventory Light-Based Shelf Monitoring
14.3.14.value: Detects empty or partially depleted shelves in real-time by monitoring light pattern changes in high-bay storage.

14.3.15.title: Structural Health Monitoring via Vibration Baseline Drift
14.3.15.value: Detects early-stage structural degradation (loose bolts, bearing wear) before catastrophic failure by monitoring vibration signature drift.

14.3.16.title: Door Open/Close Detection via Accelerometer Tilt
14.3.16.value: Monitors facility access by detecting door swing events without motion sensors or contact switches.

14.3.17.title: Equipment Alignment & Vibration Analysis via Gyroscope
14.3.17.value: Monitors rotational alignment of rotating equipment to predict misalignment-induced failures.

14.3.18.title: Sound Frequency Analysis for Equipment Signatures
14.3.18.value: Identifies equipment degradation by detecting shifts in characteristic sound frequencies (bearing wear, compressor blade damage).

14.3.19.title: Multi-Sensor Environmental Baseline & Drift Detection
14.3.19.value: Detects sensor failures, calibration drift, or environmental changes by correlating expected relationships between temperature, humidity, pressure, and light.

14.3.20.title: Pressure Monitoring for Cleanroom Compliance
14.3.20.value: Ensures pharmaceutical and semiconductor cleanroom integrity by verifying positive pressure differentials between zones.

14.3.21.title: HVAC Duct Pressure & Velocity Monitoring
14.3.21.value: Monitors HVAC filter clogging and airflow efficiency by tracking duct static pressure trends.

14.3.22.title: Weather Station Data Integration & Altitude Compensation
14.3.22.value: Provides pressure-altitude data for facility environmental baselines and corrects sensor readings for elevation changes.

14.3.23.title: Custom Python Container for Data Transformation & Enrichment
14.3.23.value: Pre-processes edge sensor data locally before forwarding to Splunk, reducing bandwidth and enabling offline analytics.

14.3.24.title: BACnet-to-MQTT Protocol Gateway Container
14.3.24.value: Bridges legacy BACnet-based building control systems with modern MQTT/Splunk pipeline without expensive protocol gateway hardware.

14.3.25.title: Local Alerting & GPIO Relay Control Container
14.3.25.value: Enables immediate equipment shutdown or alarm triggering at the edge without cloud latency, critical for safety-critical systems.

14.3.26.title: Edge Analytics Container for Rolling Statistics & Threshold Logic
14.3.26.value: Computes advanced analytics (moving averages, percentiles, trend detection) locally, reducing cloud computation burden.

14.3.27.title: BLE Beacon Asset Tracking & Presence Detection
14.3.27.value: Tracks valuable equipment or personnel location within facility using low-cost BLE tags without requiring dedicated asset management infrastructure.

14.3.28.title: USB Camera Barcode & QR Code Scanning Container
14.3.28.value: Automates material tracking and inventory verification by scanning barcodes/QR codes at the edge without manual entry.

14.3.29.title: Audio Classification for Anomalous Sound Detection
14.3.29.value: Detects equipment distress (compressor cavitation, bearing squeal, motor whine) by classifying sound types without FFT spectral analysis.

14.3.30.title: Predictive Maintenance via NPU-Based Model Inference
14.3.30.value: Predicts equipment failures (bearing degradation, motor insulation breakdown) 7-30 days in advance using on-device ML inference.

14.3.31.title: OPC-UA Tag Browsing & Change Detection
14.3.31.value: Monitors PLC tag changes in real-time and alerts on unexpected data type or value changes indicating program modification or malfunction.

14.3.32.title: Modbus TCP Register Monitoring for Industrial Equipment
14.3.32.value: Monitors equipment operational parameters via Modbus registers without requiring specialized data collection agents.

14.3.33.title: Multi-Protocol Sensor Fusion (OPC-UA + MQTT + Built-in)
14.3.33.value: Correlates data from heterogeneous sources (PLC via OPC-UA, IoT devices via MQTT, internal sensors) to identify root causes of anomalies.

14.3.34.title: Protocol Gateway Health & Connectivity Monitoring
14.3.34.value: Tracks OPC-UA/Modbus/MQTT gateway uptime and connection quality to prevent silent data loss.

14.3.35.title: Industrial Alarm Management via OPC-UA
14.3.35.value: Centralizes alarm processing from multiple PLCs via OPC-UA Alarms & Events service, preventing missed critical alerts.

14.3.36.title: Energy Meter Integration via Modbus TCP
14.3.36.value: Monitors power consumption and demand charges in real-time to identify energy waste and optimize utility costs.

14.3.37.title: PLC Program Change Detection via OPC-UA Timestamp Monitoring
14.3.37.value: Detects unauthorized or accidental PLC program modifications by tracking program last-edit timestamp changes.

14.3.38.title: SCADA HMI Event Capture & Operator Action Logging
14.3.38.value: Audits all HMI operator actions (setpoint changes, equipment starts/stops) for compliance and root cause analysis.

14.3.39.title: Multi-Device Fleet Firmware Version Compliance
14.3.39.value: Ensures all Edge Hubs in a fleet run current firmware versions to maintain security and feature parity.

14.3.40.title: Device Location Tracking via GNSS
14.3.40.value: Monitors Edge Hub physical location for mobile/outdoor deployments to verify proper coverage and detect theft/unauthorized movement.

14.3.41.title: Cellular Connectivity Quality & Signal Strength Monitoring
14.3.41.value: Tracks LTE/4G signal strength and network latency to predict connectivity issues and plan network upgrades.

14.3.42.title: Edge Hub Resource Capacity Planning & CPU/Memory Utilization
14.3.42.value: Prevents Edge Hub performance degradation and data loss by tracking resource utilization and planning for container resource allocation.

14.3.43.title: Configuration Drift Detection Across Fleet
14.3.43.value: Ensures all Edge Hubs in a fleet maintain consistent configuration (MQTT topics, OPC-UA endpoints, polling intervals) to prevent data inconsistencies.

14.3.44.title: Local Backlog Monitoring & Data Loss Prevention
14.3.44.value: Prevents silent data loss by monitoring local SQLite backlog capacity and alerting before data is discarded.

14.3.45.title: USB Camera People Counting for Occupancy & Capacity Management
14.3.45.value: Enables real-time facility occupancy tracking and automatic alerts when spaces exceed safe capacity thresholds.

14.3.46.title: USB Camera Visual Inspection for Manufacturing Defects
14.3.46.value: Automates defect detection on assembly lines by running visual inspection models on captured images without human intervention.

14.3.47.title: Custom Python Container for API Integration & Data Enrichment
14.3.47.value: Integrates Edge Hub data with external APIs (weather, commodity prices, inventory systems) to enrich sensor context without cloud latency.

14.3.48.title: Pressure & Humidity Sensor Correlation for Leakage Detection
14.3.48.value: Detects water leaks or condensation damage early by correlating pressure drop with humidity rise in sealed enclosures.

14.3.49.title: Sound Level & Frequency Band Monitoring for Regulatory Compliance
14.3.49.value: Monitors workplace noise levels to ensure OSHA compliance (90 dB over 8 hours) and tracks frequency bands for hearing loss risk.

14.3.50.title: Accelerometer-Based Fall Detection & Impact Monitoring
14.3.50.value: Detects equipment falls or impacts (e.g., dropped sensors, dropped parts on conveyor) to trigger automatic alerts and prevent asset loss.

14.3.51.title: Temperature & Humidity Sensor Calibration Drift Detection
14.3.51.value: Detects when sensors exceed acceptable calibration drift to trigger preventive recalibration and ensure measurement accuracy.

14.3.52.title: Light Sensor Ambient Light Level Anomaly Detection
14.3.52.value: Detects sudden lighting failures or unauthorized facility access by monitoring ambient light level anomalies.

14.3.53.title: Vibration Magnitude Threshold Monitoring for Equipment Protection
14.3.53.value: Protects precision equipment from damage by triggering automatic shutdowns when vibration exceeds safe operating thresholds.

14.3.54.title: Multi-Zone Temperature Gradient Monitoring for Optimal Environment
14.3.54.value: Monitors temperature gradients across facility zones to optimize HVAC distribution and detect unequal cooling/heating.

14.3.55.title: Acoustic Anomaly Detection for Equipment Health Assessment
14.3.55.value: Identifies subtle equipment changes (bearing looseness, gearbox wear) by detecting acoustic signature shifts without manual FFT analysis.

14.3.56.title: MQTT Topic Latency & Message Loss Monitoring
14.3.56.value: Ensures MQTT message delivery reliability by tracking topic latency and detecting lost or delayed messages.

14.3.57.title: Temperature Sensor Response Time Validation & Lag Detection
14.3.57.value: Validates temperature sensor response time to ensure rapid detection of thermal events (e.g., fire detection latency < 30 seconds).

14.4.1.title: Smart Sensor Fleet Health
14.4.1.value: IoT sensors with low batteries or offline status create monitoring gaps. Fleet management ensures comprehensive coverage.

14.4.2.title: Environmental Monitoring
14.4.2.value: Distributed environmental sensors provide early warning of conditions that could damage equipment or inventory.

14.4.3.title: Asset Tracking
14.4.3.value: Real-time asset location reduces search time, prevents loss, and enables utilization optimization.

14.4.4.title: Home Automation Monitoring
14.4.4.value: Smart home monitoring provides energy usage insights, security awareness, and automation troubleshooting.

14.4.5.title: IoT Device Firmware Compliance
14.4.5.value: IoT devices are frequently targeted for botnets. Outdated firmware creates network security risks.

15.1.1.title: UPS Battery Health
15.1.1.value: UPS battery degradation is the single largest cause of unprotected power events. Proactive replacement prevents data center outages.

15.1.2.title: PDU Power per Rack
15.1.2.value: Per-rack power monitoring prevents circuit overloads and enables efficient rack placement for new equipment.

15.1.3.title: Power Redundancy Status
15.1.3.value: Loss of A/B feed redundancy means a single power failure will cause an outage. Immediate awareness enables emergency response.

15.1.4.title: Generator Test Results
15.1.4.value: Generators are the last line of defense during extended outages. Failed tests mean they may not start when needed.

15.1.5.title: PUE Calculation
15.1.5.value: Power Usage Effectiveness is the primary data center efficiency metric. Trending drives energy optimization and sustainability goals.

15.1.6.title: Circuit Breaker Trips
15.1.6.value: Breaker trips cause immediate power loss to affected equipment. Detection enables rapid response and root cause investigation.

15.2.1.title: Temperature Monitoring per Zone
15.2.1.value: Data center temperature exceedances risk equipment damage and unplanned shutdowns. Per-zone monitoring localizes issues.

15.2.2.title: Humidity Monitoring
15.2.2.value: Low humidity causes ESD risk; high humidity causes condensation. Maintaining 40-60% RH protects equipment.

15.2.3.title: CRAC/CRAH Unit Health
15.2.3.value: Cooling unit failures can cause rapid temperature rise. Monitoring operational status enables immediate response and failover.

15.2.4.title: Hot Aisle Temperature Trending
15.2.4.value: Hot aisle trends indicate cooling efficiency and capacity margin. Rising trends signal approaching cooling limits.

15.2.5.title: Water Leak Detection
15.2.5.value: Water in a data center causes immediate equipment damage and potential electrical hazards. Seconds matter in detection.

15.2.6.title: Cooling Capacity Planning
15.2.6.value: Trending cooling load vs capacity ensures adequate cooling for current and planned equipment deployments.

15.3.1.title: Badge Access Audit
15.3.1.value: Complete badge access audit trail is required for compliance (SOC2, PCI-DSS) and supports security investigations.

15.3.2.title: After-Hours Access Alerts
15.3.2.value: Data center access outside business hours requires additional scrutiny. Alerts ensure authorized personnel are verified.

15.3.3.title: Tailgating Detection
15.3.3.value: Tailgating bypasses access control, allowing unauthorized entry. Detection supports physical security integrity.

15.3.4.title: Camera System Health
15.3.4.value: Offline cameras create security blind spots. Monitoring ensures continuous surveillance coverage.

15.3.5.title: Cabinet Door Monitoring
15.3.5.value: Unauthorized cabinet access could indicate tampering. Door sensors provide granular physical security for critical racks.

16.1.1.title: Incident Volume Trending
16.1.1.value: Incident trends reveal infrastructure stability, staffing needs, and the effectiveness of problem management.

16.1.2.title: SLA Compliance Monitoring
16.1.2.value: SLA breaches affect customer satisfaction and contractual obligations. Real-time monitoring enables intervention before breaches occur.

16.1.3.title: MTTR by Category
16.1.3.value: MTTR per category identifies where process improvements or automation would have the greatest impact.

16.1.4.title: Change Success Rate
16.1.4.value: Failed changes are the leading cause of incidents. Tracking success rate drives improvement in change management practices.

16.1.5.title: Change Collision Detection
16.1.5.value: Overlapping changes on related systems increase outage risk. Detection enables coordination and conflict resolution.

16.1.6.title: Problem Trending
16.1.6.value: Identifying recurring incident patterns that should become problems drives root cause resolution and reduces incident volume.

16.1.7.title: Ticket Reassignment Rate
16.1.7.value: High reassignment rates indicate poor routing or skills gaps. Reduction improves MTTR and customer satisfaction.

16.1.8.title: Aging Ticket Alerts
16.1.8.value: Aging tickets indicate stuck processes or forgotten issues. Alerts ensure nothing falls through the cracks.

16.1.9.title: Change-Incident Correlation
16.1.9.value: Correlating incidents with recent changes is the fastest path to root cause. Automated correlation accelerates MTTR.

16.1.10.title: Service Request Fulfillment Time
16.1.10.value: Fulfillment time metrics drive service catalog optimization and customer satisfaction. Slow fulfillment reduces adoption.

16.2.1.title: CMDB Data Quality Score
16.2.1.value: Poor CMDB data quality undermines all ITSM processes. Scoring and trending drives data quality improvement initiatives.

16.2.2.title: CI Discovery Reconciliation
16.2.2.value: CIs in the network but not in the CMDB are unmanaged risks. Reconciliation ensures CMDB completeness.

16.2.3.title: Orphaned CI Detection
16.2.3.value: CIs without owners or service mappings aren't managed during incidents, creating accountability gaps and shadow infrastructure.

16.2.4.title: Relationship Integrity Check
16.2.4.value: Accurate CI relationships enable impact analysis during incidents. Incomplete relationships undermine service mapping.

16.2.5.title: CMDB Change Audit
16.2.5.value: Tracking all CI attribute changes supports compliance auditing and helps detect unauthorized configuration changes.

16.3.1.title: Cross-Service Business Process Health Score
16.3.1.value: Individual component alerts do not communicate business impact. A BPI model aggregates the health of all components that together constitute a business capability (e.g., "Order Processing = web tier + database + payment gateway + message queue"). When any essential member fails, the business process is immediately flagged as degraded or down — mirroring Nagios BPI groups with essential member logic. Operations teams see business impact, not raw host counts.

16.3.2.title: Infrastructure Service Availability Heatmap
16.3.2.value: Provides a Nagios-style tactical overview — at a glance, which hosts and services have been available vs down, when, and for how long. Operations teams use this for SLA evidence, post-incident review, and capacity risk communication. Unlike individual alerts, the heatmap reveals systemic patterns: recurring daily outage windows, hosts with chronic flapping, services that always fail together.

17.1.1.title: NAC Authentication Trending
17.1.1.value: Authentication success/failure trends reveal infrastructure issues (certificate problems, RADIUS outages) and security events.

17.1.2.title: Endpoint Posture Failures
17.1.2.value: Non-compliant endpoints accessing the network pose security risks. Posture tracking ensures endpoint hygiene enforcement.

17.1.3.title: VLAN Assignment Audit
17.1.3.value: Dynamic VLAN assignments reflect authorization decisions. Anomalous placements may indicate policy misconfiguration or attacks.

17.1.4.title: Guest Network Usage
17.1.4.value: Guest network monitoring ensures acceptable use and identifies capacity needs. Unusual patterns may indicate abuse.

17.1.5.title: BYOD Onboarding Tracking
17.1.5.value: BYOD onboarding metrics inform mobile device management strategy and user experience optimization.

17.1.6.title: MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB)
17.1.6.value: MAB devices bypass 802.1X and rely on MAC address only. Monitoring for unauthorized MACs prevents rogue device access.

17.1.7.title: Profiling Accuracy
17.1.7.value: Accurate device profiling enables correct authorization policies. Misprofiled devices may get inappropriate access.

17.1.8.title: NAC Policy Change Audit
17.1.8.value: NAC policy changes affect network access for all devices. Unauthorized changes can create security gaps or disrupt access.

17.2.1.title: VPN Concurrent Sessions
17.2.1.value: VPN capacity planning prevents remote workers from being locked out. Trending identifies peak usage and growth patterns.

17.2.2.title: VPN Authentication Failures
17.2.2.value: Repeated VPN auth failures indicate credential attacks against the remote access perimeter, a primary attack vector.

17.2.3.title: Geo-Location Anomalies
17.2.3.value: VPN connections from unexpected countries may indicate compromised credentials being used from attacker infrastructure.

17.2.4.title: Split-Tunnel Compliance
17.2.4.value: Split-tunnel configurations affect security visibility. Ensuring compliance with tunnel policy maintains security posture.

17.2.5.title: VPN Tunnel Stability
17.2.5.value: Frequent disconnects indicate network issues, client problems, or infrastructure instability affecting user productivity.

17.2.6.title: Off-Hours VPN Access
17.2.6.value: VPN access at unusual hours may indicate compromised credentials or unauthorized activity. Alerting supports investigation.

17.2.7.title: VPN Bandwidth Consumption
17.2.7.value: Per-user bandwidth tracking identifies heavy users, guides capacity planning, and detects potential data exfiltration.

17.2.8.title: Simultaneous Session Detection
17.2.8.value: A single user with simultaneous VPN sessions from different locations strongly indicates credential compromise.

17.3.1.title: Conditional Access Enforcement
17.3.1.value: Tracks zero-trust policy enforcement decisions, ensuring consistent security without creating user friction.

17.3.2.title: Device Trust Scoring
17.3.2.value: Device trust scores drive access decisions in zero-trust architecture. Monitoring ensures devices maintain compliance.

17.3.3.title: Micro-Segmentation Audit
17.3.3.value: Micro-segmentation limits lateral movement. Audit logs validate policy enforcement and detect bypasses.

17.3.4.title: ZTNA Application Access
17.3.4.value: Per-application access patterns in ZTNA reveal usage trends, security risks, and application performance issues.

17.3.5.title: Posture Assessment Trending
17.3.5.value: Endpoint posture compliance rates over time measure security improvement and identify persistent non-compliance areas.

17.3.6.title: Policy Drift Detection
17.3.6.value: Zero-trust policies require continuous validation. Drift from baseline configuration introduces security gaps.

18.1.1.title: Fabric Health Score Monitoring
18.1.1.value: ACI fabric health scores provide a single-pane view of overall data center network health. Monitoring these scores lets you catch degradation before it impacts workloads, correlate health drops with specific faults, and maintain SLA compliance across your data center fabric.

18.1.2.title: Fault Trending by Severity
18.1.2.value: ACI faults are the primary operational signal from the fabric. Trending faults by severity helps identify worsening conditions, recurring hardware issues, and configuration problems before they cascade into outages.

18.1.3.title: Endpoint Mobility Tracking
18.1.3.value: Endpoint mobility in ACI tracks workload movement across leaf switches. Anomalous mobility (rapid moves, unexpected locations) can indicate misconfigurations, loops, or security issues like MAC spoofing.

18.1.4.title: Contract/Filter Hit Analysis
18.1.4.value: ACI contracts control EPG-to-EPG communication. Analyzing contract hits reveals traffic patterns, identifies overly permissive or unused contracts, and helps validate micro-segmentation policies are working as designed.

18.1.5.title: Tenant Configuration Audit
18.1.5.value: Configuration changes in ACI tenants (BDs, EPGs, contracts) are a leading cause of outages. Auditing all changes provides accountability, supports compliance, and enables rapid rollback identification when issues occur.

18.1.6.title: Leaf/Spine Interface Utilization
18.1.6.value: Fabric link saturation causes packet drops and application latency. Monitoring leaf/spine interface utilization identifies hotspots, validates ECMP distribution, and supports capacity planning for fabric expansion.

18.1.7.title: APIC Cluster Health
18.1.7.value: APIC controllers manage the entire ACI fabric. Cluster health issues (split-brain, leader election, convergence problems) can cause fabric-wide configuration and policy failures. Monitoring APIC cluster state is essential for fabric reliability.

18.2.1.title: Distributed Firewall Rule Hits
18.2.1.value: NSX Distributed Firewall (DFW) runs on every hypervisor, providing east-west traffic control. Monitoring rule hits validates security policy effectiveness, identifies unused rules for cleanup, and detects policy violations in real time.

18.2.2.title: Micro-Segmentation Enforcement
18.2.2.value: NSX micro-segmentation is a key Zero Trust control. Monitoring enforcement validates that workloads are properly isolated, detects lateral movement attempts, and proves compliance with segmentation policies during audits.

18.2.3.title: Logical Switch Health
18.2.3.value: NSX logical switches and routers form the virtual network fabric. Monitoring their operational status ensures VM connectivity and helps identify overlay network issues before they impact applications.

18.2.4.title: NSX Edge Performance
18.2.4.value: NSX Edge nodes handle north-south traffic, load balancing, and NAT. Performance bottlenecks on Edge nodes directly impact application availability and throughput for any workload communicating outside the NSX fabric.

18.2.5.title: Transport Node Connectivity
18.2.5.value: Transport nodes are the hypervisors participating in the NSX overlay. Tunnel failures between transport nodes cause VM-to-VM communication loss across hosts, directly impacting application availability.

18.3.1.title: Cilium/Calico Network Policy Monitoring
18.3.1.value: Kubernetes CNI network policies enforce pod-to-pod communication rules. Monitoring policy enforcement validates that micro-segmentation is working in containerized environments, critical for multi-tenant clusters and compliance.

18.3.2.title: OpenStack Neutron Events
18.3.2.value: Neutron manages virtual networking in OpenStack. Tracking network operations (creation, modification, deletion) provides change audit, helps troubleshoot connectivity issues, and identifies unauthorized network modifications.

18.3.3.title: SDN Controller Health
18.3.3.value: SDN controllers are the brain of software-defined networks. Controller outages or cluster consensus failures can cause network-wide disruption. Monitoring controller health ensures the control plane remains available and consistent.

19.1.1.title: Blade/Rack Server Health
19.1.1.value: UCS blade and rack servers host critical workloads. Monitoring component health (CPU, memory, PSU, fans) enables proactive hardware replacement before failures cause VM outages and unplanned downtime.

19.1.2.title: Service Profile Compliance
19.1.2.value: UCS service profiles define the identity of compute resources. Non-compliant associations indicate configuration drift, failed hardware migrations, or policy violations that can impact workload performance and security.

19.1.3.title: Firmware Compliance
19.1.3.value: Running inconsistent firmware across UCS creates compatibility issues and security vulnerabilities. Tracking firmware versions enables compliance reporting, patch planning, and ensures consistency across the compute fleet.

19.1.4.title: Fault Trending by Severity
19.1.4.value: UCS fault trends reveal systemic hardware issues, environmental problems, or configuration problems across the compute fleet. Rising fault counts indicate deteriorating conditions requiring proactive attention.

19.1.5.title: FI Port Channel Health
19.1.5.value: Fabric Interconnects are the network gateway for all UCS compute. Port-channel failures reduce bandwidth or cause complete loss of connectivity, impacting every workload in the UCS domain.

19.1.6.title: Power and Thermal Monitoring
19.1.6.value: UCS power and thermal data helps optimize data center capacity planning, detect cooling failures before overheating causes server throttling, and track energy efficiency metrics for sustainability reporting.

19.2.1.title: Cluster Health Monitoring
19.2.1.value: HCI cluster health directly determines workload availability. Monitoring overall cluster state, node availability, and service health enables rapid response to degradation before it impacts VMs and applications running on the cluster.

19.2.2.title: Storage Pool Capacity
19.2.2.value: HCI storage pools are shared across all workloads. Running out of storage capacity causes VM provisioning failures, snapshot failures, and ultimately VM crashes. Proactive monitoring and forecasting prevents capacity emergencies.

19.2.3.title: Storage I/O Latency
19.2.3.value: Storage latency directly impacts application performance on HCI. Elevated latency affects all VMs on the cluster. Early detection of latency spikes enables workload rebalancing or troubleshooting before user impact escalates.

19.2.4.title: Node Performance Balance
19.2.4.value: HCI relies on balanced workload distribution across nodes. Imbalanced nodes lead to hotspots where some nodes are overloaded while others are underutilized, reducing overall cluster efficiency and increasing failure risk.

19.2.5.title: Disk Failure Tracking
19.2.5.value: Disk failures in HCI trigger data rebuild operations that consume cluster resources and temporarily reduce resilience. Tracking failures enables rapid replacement, monitoring rebuild progress, and assessing the cluster's ability to tolerate additional failures.

19.2.6.title: Replication Factor Compliance
19.2.6.value: HCI data resilience depends on maintaining the configured replication factor (RF2/RF3). Non-compliant replication means data loss risk if additional failures occur. Monitoring RF compliance is essential for data protection assurance.

19.2.7.title: CVM (Controller VM) Health
19.2.7.value: Nutanix Controller VMs manage all storage I/O on each node. CVM failures cause I/O to redirect to other nodes, impacting performance. Monitoring CVM health ensures the HCI control plane remains operational across all nodes.

20.1.1.title: Daily Spend Trending
20.1.1.value: Cloud costs can spiral without visibility. Daily spend trending by service, account, and tag provides the financial governance foundation — enabling teams to understand where money goes, spot trends early, and make informed optimization decisions.

20.1.2.title: Cost Anomaly Detection
20.1.2.value: Unexpected cost spikes from runaway instances, misconfigured autoscaling, or crypto-mining attacks can generate thousands in charges within hours. Automated anomaly detection catches these events before they become budget disasters.

20.1.3.title: Reserved Instance Utilization
20.1.3.value: Reserved Instances and Savings Plans represent upfront commitments. Monitoring utilization ensures you're getting value from these purchases. Low utilization means wasted money; gaps in coverage mean missed savings opportunities.

20.1.4.title: Idle Resource Identification
20.1.4.value: Idle resources (running but unused instances, unattached volumes, unused load balancers) are pure waste. Identifying and eliminating them is the quickest path to cloud cost savings, often yielding 20-30% reduction.

20.1.5.title: Budget Threshold Alerting
20.1.5.value: Budget alerts prevent overspend by notifying stakeholders at defined thresholds (50%, 75%, 90%, 100%). Combined with forecast-based alerts, teams can take corrective action before exceeding approved budgets.

20.1.6.title: Cost Allocation by Team
20.1.6.value: Breaking down cloud costs by team/department via tagging creates accountability and enables chargeback/showback. Teams that see their own costs make better optimization decisions, driving organization-wide cost efficiency.

20.1.7.title: Spot/Preemptible Instance Tracking
20.1.7.value: Spot instances offer significant savings (60-90%) but can be interrupted. Tracking interruptions, savings achieved, and workload placement ensures teams maximize savings while maintaining application resilience.

20.1.8.title: Data Transfer Cost Analysis
20.1.8.value: Data transfer costs are often the most surprising cloud bill item. Inter-region, cross-AZ, and internet egress charges add up quickly. Identifying the biggest transfer flows enables architectural optimization to reduce costs significantly.

20.2.1.title: Compute Capacity Forecasting
20.2.1.value: Running out of compute capacity causes provisioning failures and performance degradation. Forecasting when CPU and memory will be exhausted enables proactive procurement or scaling, avoiding emergency purchases at premium cost.

20.2.2.title: Storage Growth Forecasting
20.2.2.value: Storage procurement has lead times. Forecasting growth trends enables timely ordering of additional capacity, preventing the emergency of running out of storage space that causes application outages and data loss.

20.2.3.title: Network Bandwidth Trending
20.2.3.value: Network bandwidth constraints cause application latency and packet loss. Trending WAN/LAN utilization enables planned upgrades during maintenance windows rather than emergency bandwidth additions during business-impacting congestion.

20.2.4.title: License Utilization Tracking
20.2.4.value: Software licenses represent significant IT spend. Tracking usage vs entitlements identifies under-licensed risks (compliance violations) and over-licensed waste (unnecessary spend). Right-sizing licenses can save 15-30% of software costs.

20.2.5.title: Right-Sizing Recommendations
20.2.5.value: Over-provisioned VMs and instances waste compute and money. Right-sizing analysis compares actual resource usage against allocated resources, identifying instances that can be downsized without impacting performance — typically saving 20-40%.

20.2.6.title: Database Growth Projection
20.2.6.value: Databases that run out of space cause application outages. Forecasting database growth enables proactive storage expansion, archive planning, and helps DBAs plan maintenance windows for data lifecycle operations.

20.2.7.title: Seasonal Capacity Modeling
20.2.7.value: Many businesses have predictable seasonal patterns (retail holidays, fiscal year-end, enrollment periods). Building seasonal capacity models ensures infrastructure scales proactively for peak periods rather than reactively during customer-impacting events.

20.2.8.title: IP Address Space Utilization
20.2.8.value: IP address exhaustion causes provisioning failures for new VMs, containers, and services. Monitoring IP pool utilization across subnets and VLANs enables proactive network planning and avoids emergency re-addressing projects.
